name: Eminem

earnings.assumptions: Estimate assumes heavy evergreen streaming and meaningful writer participation on core songs, offset by major-label master splits on parts of the catalog.

ownership.master_owner: Mix of label-controlled masters and artist-affiliated royalty participation
ownership.publishing_owner: Writing and publishing participation appears to remain a major value driver
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumed in this estimate
ownership.notes: For major catalogs like Eminem's, the difference between gross music revenue and artist-side take-home can be large, but songwriter economics materially improve the artist cut.

bio: Eminem built one of rap's most replayed catalogs, with songs that continue to generate streams, sync value, and cultural rediscovery.
notable_for: Lyrical intensity, blockbuster singles, and multi-generational catalog staying power.
career_highlight: His early 2000s material still performs at mainstream scale decades after release.
revenue_drivers: Evergreen streaming from major singles keeps the catalog active year-round.
Publishing and master rights on landmark tracks continue to generate revenue.
Film and nostalgia-driven sync usage help iconic songs keep earning.
